title: alnus viridis subsp. fruticosa
intro: Alnus viridis subsp. fruticosa, also known as the dwarf green alder, is a subspecies of the alder tree, characterized by its smaller size and compact growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, thanks to the presence of salicylic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Alnus viridis subsp. fruticosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its role in reducing fever and promoting sweating. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
